## Transcript

### I ran a free AI model in my browser — here's how

**0:00** · This image was generated by an open source AI model running live right now.

**0:05** · On a completely free hardware, no API keys, no subscription, and no GPU of my own, just a browser tap.

**0:12** · And in about 15 minutes, I'm going to show you how to exactly do this yourself, and more importantly, how to turn this into a REST API, which you can then plug in into any of your projects.

**0:23** · Because here's the thing, most tutorials show you how to use AI, but this one here shows you how to to run them.

**0:30** · There's a massive difference.

**0:31** · When you run the model yourself, you control it, you can expose it as an API, you can integrate it into your app, you can customize it, you can extend it, and do all of this without paying a single dollar in API cost.

**0:45** · And yes, we are going to talk about the models that tend to work, because that is actually the most useful part any developer would love watching.

### What is HuggingFace? The GitHub of AI explained

**0:53** · Now, think of Hugging Face as the GitHub of AI.

**0:55** · It's where researchers and companies publish their models.

**0:59** · Everything from image generators to language models to speech recognition.

**1:04** · Around 3 million models are hosted right now on Hugging Face, most of them free to download and use.

### What are HuggingFace Spaces?

**1:09** · Now hugging face has something called a spaces.

**1:12** · These are basically hosted demo apps built on top of models.

**1:16** · Someone takes a model, wraps it up in a radio or streamlit UI and hugging face hosts it for you.

**1:23** · You can go to the space URL and just use the model in your browser.

**1:28** · No setup and no code.

### Why free Spaces fail developers (no GPU, no API, goes to sleep)

**1:29** · But here is where it gets interesting for developers.

**1:32** · Spaces on free tier run on shared CPU hardware, that means slower interfaces, sometimes even more than One minute, and critically, you can't call space as an API in any reliable way.

**1:45** · There is no guaranteed uptime, no SLA, and no control over the runtime.

**1:50** · If the space goes to sleep, your app breaks.

**1:53** · And there's another problem: a lot of what you will find on spaces isn't actually a model at all.

**1:59** · It is just a demo app wrapping someone else's model.

**2:03** · We hid this ourselves while researching this video, but more on this later.

### What is Google Colab and why it beats Spaces

**2:08** · Google Colab is a free hosted Jupyter notebook environment, you write Python in your browser and it runs on Google's server.

**2:16** · The key thing for us is the free tier that gives you access to an Nvidia T4 GPU with 15 gigs of VRAM for free, no credit card.

### The real power: turning any model into a REST API with FastAPI + ngrok

**2:27** · The real power of Colab for a developer isn't just running the model.

**2:32** · In fact, it is that you can expose the model as a proper REST API using FastAPI and NgRock and of a sudden you have a real endpoint that you can call from your React app or from your mobile app from anywhere and that is what we are going to build today.

**2:48** · So ngrock creates a secure tunnel from the collab machine to a public URL so your model running on Google's GPU becomes callable from anywhere in the world with a single post request.

### The model we're using: Stable Diffusion XL Base 1.0

**3:02** · So the model that we are using today is Stable Diffusion XL Base 1 by Stability AI.

**3:08** · This is one of the most well-known image generation models in the world.

**3:13** · It generates 1024 by 1024 images.

**3:17** · It uses a dual text encoder architecture for much better prompt understanding.

**3:23** · And most importantly, it fits into our free T4 GPU.
